NIS 2 `security in … maintenance of network and information systems, including vulnerability handling and disclosure` (Art. 21(2)(e) third clause) — entity-side partial-coverage of CI/CD via the maintenance dimension (T3-vs-text gap: OJ does not mention `pipeline`, `CI`, or `CD` — the partial coverage is realised through the 3-phase coordinated lifecycle via the maintenance phase; AEGIS taxonomy §4.1 marks NIS 2 as sole authority with the T3-vs-text caveat).

## 1. Cross-Regulation Analysis [VERBATIM]


> **Domain analysis:** [D-07.3 CI/CD Pipeline Security](../../CrossRegulation/DomainAnalysis/D-07_Secure-Development/D-07.3.md)

### D-07.3 CI/CD Pipeline Security

<!-- participants: NIS2, CRA -->

**Participants (from CRDA):** NIS2, CRA

**Sole-authority note:** AEGIS taxonomy §4.1 marks NIS 2 as sole authority for D-07.3 CI/CD Pipeline Security (T3-vs-text gap: OJ Art. 21(2)(e) does not mention `pipeline`, `CI`, or `CD`). NIS 2 provides only partial coverage via the maintenance dimension. CRA Annex I Part II (7)+(8) covers update distribution (delivery path), not CI/CD build-time controls.

#### Pair: NIS2 ↔ CRA

**NIS2 article (verbatim, Art. 21(2)(e) third clause):**
> "[Member States shall ensure that essential and important entities take appropriate and proportionate technical, operational and organisational measures to … ] (e) … security in the maintenance of network and information systems, including vulnerability handling and disclosure." — Art. 21(2)(e) third clause NIS 2 (per SR-NIS2-028)

**CRA article (verbatim, Annex I Part II (7)+(8)):**
> "The manufacturer shall … (7) securely distribute updates for the product with digital elements in a timely manner, including, where appropriate, through automatic updates; (8) ensure that updates are disseminated free of charge, are clearly described, contain information on the security improvements and on the actions the user should take." — CRA Annex I Part II (7)+(8)

**Scope comparison:**
- NIS 2 scope: entity-level maintenance-phase security including vulnerability handling (partial coverage of CI/CD via maintenance dimension).
- CRA scope: product-level update-distribution delivery path (Part II (7)+(8)) — not CI/CD build-time controls (signed artefacts, build-agent integrity, ephemeral build environments).
- Scope overlap: **N (typically)** — NIS 2 covers entity-side CI/CD pipeline; CRA covers product-side update delivery path.

**Trigger comparison:**
- NIS 2 trigger: NIS maintenance activity.
- CRA trigger: manufacturer update distribution.
- Trigger alignment: **DIFFERENT** (entity side vs. maker side).

**Threshold/timeline comparison:**
- NIS 2: no quantitative cadence.
- CRA: `without delay` (Part II (8)) — qualitative.
- Tension: **NONE**.

**Recipient comparison:**
- NIS 2: management body, competent authority.
- CRA: MSA, users.

**Content template comparison:**
- NIS 2: not codified for content.
- CRA: Annex VII §2 design/development documentation.

**Classified relationship (from CRDA):** COMPLEMENTARY
**Verified relationship (after OJ-text analysis):** **SAME — COMPLEMENTARY**
**Reasoning:** NIS 2 covers the entity-side CI/CD pipeline (entity deploying NIS into production); CRA covers the product-side update delivery path (manufacturer distributing security updates). The two regimes meet at the supplier-customer interface — NIS 2 procurement gate (SR-NIS2-026) references CRA-conformant products, and CRA Part II (7)/(8) update-distribution obligations discharge the NIS 2 supplier-side patch pathway. The CI/CD pipeline security sub-domain has no dedicated OJ mandate in any of the 5 regulations.

**Scope-disjoint test:** Does this pair actually overlap on a single event at a single party? **N (typically)** — different objects (entity-side CI/CD pipeline vs. product-side update delivery path), different actors. Pair is substantively scope-disjoint; CRDA "complementary" is a taxonomy artifact.

**Downstream implication (Phase 1C Doc 07):** Phase 1 entities must construct CI/CD baseline from industry frameworks (NIST SP 800-204, OWASP CI/CD Top 10, SLSA). Pipeline-architecture controls (signed artefacts, build-agent integrity, ephemeral build environments) are industry best-practice, not regulatory mandate.

### Downstream implication for Phase 1C Doc 07

A company with ≥2 applicable regulations in scope of D-07.3 — with overlapping target-object scope-determiners on the same Conditional-Y integrated stack — operates a **2-layer CI/CD-pipeline-security architecture** with explicit layered hand-offs and **industry-framework build-time baseline** as the operational instrument. The high-level SO (`SO-D-07.3.HL`) describes the common CI/CD-pipeline-security duty (build-time control baseline + update-distribution delivery-path security — typically built from industry frameworks NIST SP 800-204 + OWASP CI/CD Top 10 + SLSA since no OJ clause prescribes CI/CD build-time controls); the per-reg perspective is preserved in the two sub-SOs (target object, trigger, anchor, threshold, recipient, evidence template). **The 0 conditional emergent tensions are documented as a layered reconciliation**, with the 2-layer CI/CD-pipeline-security architecture (NIS 2 entity-side partial-coverage via maintenance dimension [Art. 21(2)(e) third clause + T3-vs-text gap + AEGIS-taxonomy sole-authority assignment] → CRA product-side update-delivery-path partial-coverage via Annex I Part II (7)+(8)) operationalising the inter-reg hand-off at the supplier-customer boundary of the Conditional-Y integrated stack. The high-level SO does NOT collapse the two target objects into a single CI/CD-pipeline architecture; the per-reg perspective on target object, trigger, anchor, threshold, recipient, and evidence template is preserved verbatim. Phase 1C Doc 07 documents the **CI/CD-pipeline-security strategy**: (1) a 2-layer CI/CD-pipeline-security architecture with explicit target-object hand-offs (NIS 2 Art. 21(2)(e) third clause entity-side maintenance-dimension partial-coverage with T3-vs-text gap → CRA Annex I Part II (7)+(8) product-side update-delivery-path partial-coverage) — **the entity-side NIS 2 procurement gate (`SR-NIS2-026`) + the product-side CRA Part II (7)/(8) update-distribution evidence meet at the supplier-customer boundary on the Conditional-Y integrated stack**; (2) the **NIS 2 T3-vs-text gap as a structural Layer 2 review item** (no OJ mandate for CI/CD build-time controls; `SR-NIS2-028` partial-coverage via the maintenance dimension; AEGIS taxonomy §4.1 sole-authority assignment with T3-vs-text caveat — either (a) the AEGIS taxonomy §4.1 should be clarified, or (b) the T3 taxonomy should be revised to remove the CI/CD-pipeline attribution from NIS 2 Art. 21(2)(e)); (3) the **CRA Annex I Part II (7)+(8) coverage-scope caveat as a structural Layer 2 review item** (no OJ CI/CD build-time mandate; partial-coverage cross-ref via SO-CRA-009 / `SR-CRA-009`; CRA covers product-side update delivery path only); (4) the **industry-framework CI/CD build-time baseline** (NIST SP 800-204, OWASP CI/CD Top 10, SLSA) as the operational instrument on the typical-case integrated stack where neither regulator provides an OJ CI/CD build-time mandate — the entity builds the CI/CD build-time baseline from industry frameworks and demonstrates compliance against each regulator's partial-coverage dimension independently on the typical-case N scope-disjoint baseline, or against the integrated supplier-customer-boundary evidence on the Conditional-Y integrated stack.

**End of file.**

## Decisions (CORR-029)

### Orphan SR justifications

**AI_Act**: 1 SR (SR-AIACT-023) targets D-07.3 but no AI_Act SO exists. Per source MD line 109, 'AI_Act (out-of-scope). AI Act does not impose CI/CD mandate. SR-AIACT-023 (Art. 55(1)(a) GPAI evaluation with adversarial testing) has testing-and-validation dimensions but not CI/CD build-time controls'. SR-AIACT-023 is preserved as a partial-coverage cross-ref (testing-and-validation dimension touches the CI/CD pipeline evidence layer) but is not anchored to a D-07.3 AI_Act SO because AI_Act does not impose a CI/CD-pipeline obligation. CORR-030 may re-anchor SR-AIACT-023 to D-07.2 (testing dimension) or to a D-08.2 (role-specific AI competence) dimension.


### Orphan SO justifications

**NIS2**: SO-D-07.3.NIS2 inherits from SO-NIS2-017 (partial via the maintenance dimension — SO-NIS2-017 routes to D-07.3 via Art. 21(2)(e) third clause). The CI/CD-pipeline-security obligation in NIS2 is preserved as a partial-coverage cross-ref to the maintenance-dimension primary in D-02.2. AEGIS taxonomy §4.1 marks NIS2 as sole authority for D-07.3 (T3-vs-text gap: OJ Art. 21(2)(e) does not mention 'pipeline', 'CI', or 'CD'). The partial coverage is via the maintenance dimension only. No standalone NIS2 SO is needed for D-07.3 because the OJ text does not prescribe CI/CD build-time controls; the obligation is operationalised through D-02.2's maintenance dimension.

**CRA**: SO-D-07.3.CRA inherits from SO-CRA-009 (partial cross-ref via Annex I Part II (7) — secure update distribution; SO-CRA-009 primary sub-domain is D-02.2 patch management with cross-ref to D-07.3 partial). The CRA CI/CD-pipeline-security obligation is preserved as a partial-coverage cross-ref to the patch-management primary in D-02.2. CRA covers the product-side update delivery path (Annex I Part II (7)+(8)) only, not CI/CD build-time controls. The CI/CD build-time baseline is supplied by industry frameworks (NIST SP 800-204 / OWASP CI/CD Top 10 / SLSA), not by the OJ text.
